summary: The Rails CSRF authenticity_token is resolved from the GOODREADS_AUTHENTICITY_TOKEN env var or stdin (shared helpers readAuthenticityToken / readAuthenticityTokenWithStdin); the --authenticity-token / --body... flags are deprecated and warn when used. Also taught client dryRun to print non-JSON (form) bodies.
reason: Greptile P2: a CSRF token passed as a CLI flag leaks into shell history and the process table (ps aux), the same risk auth.go documents for credentials-as-args. Applied to review create, review create-update, and shelf create.
validated_outcome: review/shelf create read the token from GOODREADS_AUTHENTICITY_TOKEN with no flag; passing --authenticity-token still works but prints a deprecation warning to stderr.
